description:
You are an Earth Science teacher at an OLPC-equipped school developing a lesson on interpreting climate data. The TurtleBlocks activity is open with a blank canvas.

Your task is to build a TurtleBlocks program that draws a 5-bar vertical bar chart visualizing NOAA average monthly precipitation data for Seattle, WA.

Requirements:
1. Draw 5 vertical bars (rectangles) from left to right representing January through May.
2. Use these exact height values for the 5 bars (1 unit = 1 pixel):
   - Bar 1 (Jan): 142
   - Bar 2 (Feb): 89
   - Bar 3 (Mar): 95
   - Bar 4 (Apr): 70
   - Bar 5 (May): 48
3. The width of every bar must be exactly 50 pixels.
4. The horizontal spacing (gap) between each bar must be exactly 20 pixels (meaning the start of the next bar is 70 pixels from the start of the previous one).
5. Bars must be drawn growing upward from a common baseline.

Save the completed program as /home/ga/Documents/seattle_rainfall.ta using File > Save As.
